title: Operator Decrypt CLI Output
description: Canonical output envelope for `security-service/tools/decrypt-cli`. Single-sided per feedback_operator_decrypt_cli_single_sided.md — no monolith-side sibling. Stored in tools/decrypt-cli/src/main/resources/ and validated by OperatorDecryptOutputSchemaTest on build. Schema-version bumps require a follow-up ticket; v1.0 is locked at M.0 sign-off.

schema_version.description: Output-document schema version. Bumped only via coordinated PR.

row_count.description: Total number of decrypted rows in this document. Equal to rows.length.

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings encountered during the run (e.g. partial-page truncation note when --i-understand-large-export was opted in).

operator_email.description: RFC 5322 email of the human running the CLI. Required CLI flag --operator-email.

reason.description: Free-text operator-stated reason for the decrypt. Required CLI flag --reason. Must be 16..256 chars.

argument_vector.description: Full argv as parsed. NEVER contains plaintext — only flag names and table/ID references.

correlation_id.description: Optional incident-response correlation id (typically the same UUID written into a Pagerduty / Linear ticket).

started_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the CLI process started. Recorded in the audit row.

finished_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the CLI process completed. May equal started_at for fast runs.

side.description: Which audit chain owns the OPERATOR_DECRYPT_RUN row for this invocation. Always 'security-service' for v1.0 — the monolith does not host a sibling CLI.

dry_run.description: True when --dry-run was set. Plaintext is not actually emitted; row_count + rows reflect what WOULD have been decrypted.

i_understand_large_export.description: True when the operator opted past the 10000-row / 24h hard cap with --i-understand-large-export. Recorded in audit detail_json.

i_accept_plaintext_on_disk.description: True when the operator paired --output-file with --i-accept-plaintext-on-disk. Required to emit any plaintext to durable storage.

output_destination.description: Where plaintext was written. 'stdout' is the default; 'file' requires both --output-file and --i-accept-plaintext-on-disk.

table.description: Logical table name. For v1.0 the supported tables are security-service-owned (security_keys.deks, security_keys.jwt_signing_keys, future encrypted state).

primary_key.description: Stringified primary key of the decrypted row.

dek_handle_hex.description: Hex DEK handle used to decrypt this row, when applicable (e.g. wrapped_dek_bytes). Null for DEK metadata lookups that do not unwrap anything.

key_handle.description: Opaque key handle (kid) when the row is a JWT signing key (security_keys.jwt_signing_keys).

column_decrypts.description: Decrypted column name -> decrypted plaintext value. Each value is the result of unwrap/decrypt against the security service's local KEK material. Values are intentionally typed `string` — binary outputs (e.g. raw DEK bytes) are base64-encoded by the CLI before insertion. Empty object in --dry-run.
